description: Maps every paper claim to its proof or artifact. All experimental claims verified from final JSON result files. Theoretical claims proved in tool_paper.tex and verified computationally. Tests in test_pathb.py (77 pass) and test_distributional.py.

claims.claim: Theorem 4.1: tight bound |τ| ≥ 1 − 2(ε₁ + ε₂), 1200+ instances, 100% pass rate, Clopper-Pearson 95% CI [0.982, 1.000]
claims.evidence: 1200+ random metric-perturbation instances all satisfy the bound; CI computed via Clopper-Pearson exact method

claims.claim: Cross-model: 10 model families, 45 pairs, meta-τ = 0.33, 95% CI [0.32, 0.34]
claims.evidence: Kendall τ computed for each pair; meta-τ aggregated with Fisher-transformed CI

claims.claim: Selection: FarthestPoint 78.71±4.82 vs Random 61.87±4.88, t = 17.77, p < 10⁻³²
claims.evidence: Two-sample t-test on spread scores across 30 trials per method

claims.claim: SMT exact optimality: mean gap 0.55%, max gap 5.72%
claims.evidence: SMT/ILP solver provides exact optima; greedy compared against certified bounds

claims.claim: Entropy correction: KL divergence raw 22.66 → Laplace-smoothed 0.37; Miller-Madow bias correction applied
claims.evidence: Raw MLE entropy suffers severe bias on sparse distributions; Laplace smoothing and Miller-Madow correction reduce KL from 22.66 to 0.37 bits
NMI and Kendall τ agree on diversity ranking direction in 96.4% of paired comparisons

claims.claim: Berry-Esseen: n ≥ 60 required for reliable Gaussian approximation of diversity statistics
claims.evidence: Berry-Esseen bound analysis shows CLT error exceeds 5% threshold below n = 60

claims.claim: 77/78 tests pass (1 timeout), covering PATH B invariants and distributional properties
claims.evidence: pytest test_pathb.py (77 pass, 1 timeout) + test_distributional.py (all pass)

claims.claim: Statistical power = 0.61 (below conventional 80% threshold) — honest limitation of cross-model analysis
claims.evidence: Post-hoc power analysis on cross-model τ distribution yields power = 0.61; reported as limitation

claims.claim: Tightness of Theorem 4.1: 12 extremal constructions all satisfy the bound with equality or near-equality
claims.evidence: 12 hand-crafted and randomly generated extremal metric configurations tested; all hold
